title: webview_flutter
objectives: Embed a WebView with a controller

sections.markdown: `webview_flutter` embeds a native WebView (WKWebView on iOS, WebView on Android) inside a Flutter widget. Recent versions use platform views with a controller-based API.

sections.code:
final ctrl = WebViewController()
  ..setJavaScriptMode(JavaScriptMode.unrestricted)
  ..setNavigationDelegate(NavigationDelegate(
    onNavigationRequest: (r) => allow(r.url) ? NavigationDecision.navigate : NavigationDecision.prevent,
  ))
  ..addJavaScriptChannel('Flutter', onMessageReceived: (m) => handle(m.message))
  ..loadRequest(Uri.parse(url));

sections.markdown: The WebView is a wide-open attack surface. Whitelist origins in `onNavigationRequest`, disable JS unless required, and be careful with `addJavaScriptChannel` — anything the injected page can post reaches your app.

Loading arbitrary user-supplied URLs. If a link is untrusted, open it in the system browser (`url_launcher`) — don't embed. Also: forgetting to configure cookies + iOS App Transport Security for HTTPS-only.

For SSO/OAuth in-app, prefer `flutter_web_auth_2` or an ASWebAuthenticationSession-based flow — safer + supported by IdPs. For rich interop consider `flutter_inappwebview` (more features, more surface).

flashcards.back: addJavaScriptChannel exposes a JS object whose postMessage reaches Dart.

flashcards.front: How to block navigation to untrusted URLs?

flashcards.back: flutter_web_auth_2 / ASWebAuthenticationSession over an embedded WebView.

quiz.q: For an OAuth login flow, prefer…
quiz.explain: Providers block/embargo embedded WebViews for security.

options: A JS→Dart message channel — treat inputs as untrusted
quiz.explain: Always validate/limit what the page can send.

challenges.text: Show a fixed URL in a WebView.
Bridge JS → Dart via a channel; show messages in a snackbar.
Block navigation to non-whitelisted origins; open external links in the system browser.
Implement a secure OAuth flow via flutter_web_auth_2 instead of embedded WebView.

interviewQuestions: How do you bridge JS and Dart?
How do you block untrusted navigations?
Why avoid embedded WebViews for OAuth?
How to handle cookies and downloads?
